I agree with the original conclusion. Cervical lymphadenopathy in the first trimester is a classic sign of primary toxoplasmosis infection, and the failure of spiramycin administration suggests actual exposure rather than mere suspicion. Since spiramycin is specifically indicated for preventing congenital toxoplasmosis, its ineffectiveness underscores an underlying infection, further strengthening the link to neonatal hydrocephalus and intracerebral calcifications.
